vulnerabilities.title: Complex Reentrancy in Swap Path
vulnerabilities.description: A sophisticated reentrancy vector exists in the cross-pair exchange logic where transient state is not correctly reset before external calls to exotic tokens with non-standard behavior.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Potential for incorrect balance accounting leading to fund drainage.
vulnerabilities.likelihood: LOW - Requires specific combination of illiquid tokens and malicious contracts.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement non-reentrant decorators and ensure all internal state updates occur before external calls.

vulnerabilities.title: Slippage Parameter Overflow
vulnerabilities.description: Fixed-point arithmetic during slippage calculation can overflow when dealing with extremely large trade volumes, resulting in a denial-of-service for whale transactions.
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM - Disruption of large-volume liquidity flows.
vulnerabilities.likelihood: MEDIUM - Highly likely for extreme price swings on low liquidity pairs.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use Vyper's built-in safe math and add explicit range checks for pricing scalars.

questions.question: Have the non-standard ERC20 behaviors been accounted for in the internal balance cache?
